pending.label: Della - Letter 1 variant B
pending.variant:
Philippa.

You fell asleep on my shoulder with the orange book still in your bag. I felt the corner of it against my arm.

Then the train hit a bump. Just one.


And you were gone, and the book was gone, and the warmth stayed.

I'm going to walk every carriage until it gives you back.

You can read this. I taught you that.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 2 variant A
pending.variant:
I've stopped crying. Crying is for when nothing can be done. Plenty can be done.

I have a notebook. I number the carriages and mark the ones I've searched.


I'll find my girl the same way.

And her father, if he made it aboard.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 2 variant B
pending.variant:
Philippa,

Your father went ahead to the train weeks ago, to find us a way through. Then the raiders came, and it was only us.

We ran. We came here to find him.


So I'm looking for two faces now. Yours, and his.

Two is not harder than one. It is twice the reason to keep my eyes open.

I cross out the carriages. I don't cross out the days.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 3 variant A
pending.variant:
I found a paper today.

Three words in a child's hand, and I knew them before I read them. I taught that hand its letters.


It was hers. Pip's. It said she is looking for me.

So she is alive. She is awake. She is moving, and she is looking, same as I am.


The paper was not fresh. She passed through before me. I am behind her.

But behind is not lost. Behind, I can follow.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 3 variant B
pending.variant:
Philippa. I found your note.

You wrote that you are looking for me. I have read it forty times. I have it in my coat.


You are out there, and you are still you — still looking, still walking, not sitting down to wait. That is my girl.


So here is what we do now. You leave your papers. I follow them. I am closer than I was yesterday, even if the train says otherwise.

Keep writing, Pip. I am reading every word.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 4 variant A
pending.variant:
There is a man on this train who wears a yellow coat.

I have seen men like him before — after long marches, after sieges. The train is only a longer siege.


He told me the train does not stop. He has stopped, though, in every way that counts. He mostly sleeps now.

But he looked at me when I described her. And he nodded.


He saw her. A small girl, he said, asking everyone for her mother. Would not sit, would not stay.

That is twice now the train has told me she is alive. I will not get tired of hearing it.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 4 variant B
pending.variant:
Philippa, today I met a man in a yellow coat.

He has ridden this train longer than a person should. The train has worn him thin. But there is still kindness in him.


He remembered you. A girl asking after her mother, he said, who would not sit down. He smiled when he said it. The first time I have seen this train make anyone smile.


I asked him about your father too. About Tomas. He shook his head. Nothing.

The train will give me back one of you. I refuse to believe it cannot be both.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 5 variant A
pending.variant:
I have a friend now. Her name is Soren.

I had forgotten what it costs to do this alone, until I stopped having to.


She has been on the train longer than I have years. She knows its moods. She gives away food she cannot spare and calls it nothing.

We search together now. Two sets of eyes.


She thinks I push too hard. I think she has stopped pushing at all.

For now that is a thing we can laugh about. For now we walk the same direction.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 5 variant B
pending.variant:
Philippa, I want you to know about Soren.

She found me at a low hour and did not leave. She is the first good thing this train has handed me.


She has been aboard longer than anyone I have met. She talks about a door that leads off the train. She wants me to want it.

I tell her I want only one thing, and it has your name.


When I find you, you will meet her. You will like her, I think. She knows where to find the most delicious apples.

Hold on to that, Pip. There are good people even here.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 6 variant A
pending.variant:
Pip's papers led into a dark stretch. The kind even Soren would not enter.

She told me to turn back. I told her the trail goes forward, so I go forward.


We said hard things. Then the floor gave way beneath me, and I went down into the dark between the carriages. The last thing I heard was Soren screaming my name.


But I survived. I caught a rail and climbed back up, bruised and breathing.

Not three carriages back, where I fell. With the train moving, surely not ahead.

Somewhere I had yet to travel.


I can't get back to her. The dark does not give second chances.

Whatever she believes became of me down there, I'll never get the chance to tell her otherwise.

The trail goes forward. So do I.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 6 variant B
pending.variant:
Philippa,

Your trail led into a dark stretch. She begged me not to follow it. I followed it. You were at the end of it, or might be.


We argued. The last word I gave her was a sharp one. Then the floor opened under me and I fell into the dark between the carriages, with her screaming above me.


I lived, Pip. I caught hold and climbed back, far down the train. Soren saw none of it — only me dropping into the black and not coming back.

She likely thinks I'm dead, and I can't tell her she's wrong.


If I do not meet her again, at least there is another set of eyes looking for you now.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 7 variant A
pending.variant:
I've stopped counting days. Not because I gave up — because the number stopped mattering and the looking did not.


Tomas's trail went cold a long way back. No papers. No word. I keep his name in my mouth so it doesn't get lost too.

Pip's trail I still have. Faint, but mine to follow.


I know now there may be no end to this train, and no end to the walking. I've made my peace with that. I have not made peace with stopping.

So I won't stop.


I leave my own papers now, in the carriages everyone passes. If I can't catch her, she can catch me.


A mother and a daughter, walking the same endless train, leaving each other words.

We will cross. I have decided it.

Della

pending.label: Della - Letter 7 variant B
pending.variant:
Philippa, I'm leaving this where everyone passes — the way you taught me, without knowing you taught me.


I'm still behind you. I've read your papers. You're still walking, still you, and that's all I need to keep walking too.

Your father I have not found. Hold his name with me. Tomas.


I don't know how long this train is. I've decided it doesn't matter. I'll walk it as long as it takes, and longer. I am closer than it wants me to be.


You wrote once that mums find things.

You were right. Keep writing, Pip — I am reading every word.

I will find you.

Della

pending.label: Soren - Letter 1 variant A
pending.variant:
Whoever finds this seat,

it is warm. I have only just left it.

The apple is yours. A gift you have surely earnt.

I have been aboard longer than I can count. I stopped looking for the front a long way back.


One thing, dear one. Stay out of the dark stretches.

Whatever you have lost, it is not in there. The dark keeps what it takes.

Eat something first.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 1 variant B
pending.variant:
If you are reading this, love, you are new. Or you are kind. Both wear out here.

I left the apple because feeding someone is the last thing here that still feels like winning.


I believed once I would get off. The train cured me of that.

Avoid the dark stretches. I know why now. It cost more than I had. The dark keeps what it takes.

Rest here a while.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 2 variant A
pending.variant:
For years I told people about the door.

A way off the train. Go through it, I would say, if you ever find it.

I half meant it. You give people hope you are not sure of. It is still hope.


Today I learned it is real. I will not tell you how. But I know it now.

All these years I gave away a hope I was not sure of. Now the hope is true.

If you find that door, sweetheart.

Go through it.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 2 variant B
pending.variant:
I had made my peace. Peace is only wanting nothing. It is a kind of sleep.

Then I learned the door is real, and the wanting woke up all at once.

I am awake again now. I am not certain I thank it for that.


I could be home by morning. You forget how strange that word gets. Home.

But I have never walked past a frightened face here. I will not start now.

So I will guide others to it, on my own way there.

Go through it.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 3 variant A
pending.variant:
Della.

I found you at the bottom of a bad hour. I gave you an apple. I did not leave.

It has been years since I walked beside anyone. I had forgotten the weight of it.

We search for your child together now. Two sets of eyes.


The door is real, Della. You could be home.

You shook your head. You will not leave without her.

A small girl of eight. An orange book in her bag. You taught her to read. Pip.

Eat. We walk at first light.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 3 variant B
pending.variant:
Della.

Years alone, and then you. You handed me back something I had stopped letting myself miss. A voice that answers.

Even after this train took your child, you keep walking. So I help you search. My door can wait a moment longer.


I have seen this train wear people to nothing. I am afraid it will wear you.

You push too hard. I fear you are right to.

The door will be there for the three of us. Once we find little Philippa.

Eat. We walk at first light.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 4 variant A
pending.variant:
Della.

The trail went into a dark stretch. The kind I do not enter. I begged you to turn back.

You said it goes forward, so you go forward. We said hard things. They were the last.


Then it felt like the floor gave way. I think I heard you dropped into the dark between the carriages.

I heard your scream.
Then... nothing.


I screamed your name until I had no voice, and the dark gave none of it back. The dark keeps what it takes.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 4 variant B
pending.variant:
Della.

I told you the dark stretch would kill you. You said your girl's trail led into it.

So you went in. I wanted you to live. You wanted your daughter. We were both right. That is the cruel part.


I have replayed the fall a thousand times. There is no version where I catch you.

There is no three of us now. I broke my own rule, and the dark took you. The dark keeps what it takes.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 5 variant A
pending.variant:
Della.

After the dark, I stopped. I stopped handing out apples. I stopped looking at faces. I walked, and I did not see the carriages go by.

Grief is its own dark stretch. I did not heed my own rule.


I do not know how long I have been like this. The train eats time. Too long, I think.

But today the fog lifted. I can taste an apple again. It is time, Della. I am going to the door.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 5 variant B
pending.variant:
Della.

There are people on this train who have stopped. You meet them. Their eyes are somewhere else. I always pitied them.

After the dark, I became one of them. I do not know for how long.


The train eats time. I think it ate too much of mine. I am ashamed of the apples I let go cold.

But I am awake now. I can carry the grief and still walk. So I am going to the door. It is finally time.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 6 variant A
pending.variant:
Pip.

You asked after your mother, and I knew you at once. I had walked with her, until the dark took her.

You saw only a woman with an apple. I gave it to you, and you called us friends.


You told me all about her. I went quiet.

I said your mother sounds wonderful. It came out hollow, like a thing you say but no longer believe. You are still going to find her.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 6 variant B
pending.variant:
Pip.

You had her face. I knew you before you finished asking. She walked with me until she fell into the dark and did not come up.

You are her girl, hunting for her, and you do not know she is gone.


You asked how long I had been here. I would not say. You told me of her.

I went quiet. I said she sounds wonderful. One word more was to say she is dead. So I let you keep looking. Forgive me, sweetheart.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 7 variant A
pending.variant:
Pip.

I told you about the door at last. The real way home. Go through it, I said, the moment you find it.

You said you would sit by it and wait for your mother. I said okay. This time I meant it.


I was leaving, my hand on the way home. But I will not walk out of the dark while a child waits in it.

So I am the keeper of the door now. She waits for a mother I am sure is gone, and I never tell her.

Soren

pending.label: Soren - Letter 7 variant B
pending.variant:
Pip.

I gave you the way home. The true one, at last. I told you to go through it and not look back.

You will not. You will sit by it and keep your seat for your mother. I did not argue. For once I meant the kindness.


I had my own way home in my hand. I let it go.

I will grow old at this door, giving it away and using it for no one. A child waits here for a mother who is gone, and I keep the truth I will never say.

Soren
